Package org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.rewriters

Class AbstractManualRewrites

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.internal.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.rewriters.AbstractManualRewrites
All Implemented Interfaces:
IReasoner
Direct Known Subclasses:
AndOrDistRewrites, ArithRewrites, BCompDefRewrites, CardDefRewrites, CompImgRewrites, CompUnionDistRewrites, ContImplHypRewrites, ConvRewrites, DisjunctionToImplicationRewrites, DomCompRewrites, DomDistLeftRewrites, DomDistRightRewrites, DomRanUnionDistRewrites, DoubleImplHypRewrites, EqualCardRewrites, EqualFunImgDefRewrites, EqvRewrites, FiniteDefRewrites, FunImgSimplifies, ImpAndRewrites, ImpOrRewrites, InclusionSetMinusRewrites, MinMaxDefRewrites, PartitionRewrites, RanCompRewrites, RanDistLeftRewrites, RanDistRightRewrites, RelImgUnionLeftRewrites, RelImgUnionRightRewrites, RelOvrRewrites, RemoveInclusion, RemoveInclusionUniversal, RemoveMembership, RemoveNegation, SetEqlRewrites, SetMinusRewrites, StrictInclusionRewrites, UnionInterDistRewrites
public abstract class AbstractManualRewrites extends Object implements IReasoner

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public IReasonerOutput apply(IProverSequent seq, IReasonerInput reasonerInput, IProofMonitor pm)
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Apply this reasoner to the given sequent with the given input.
      Specified by:
      apply in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      seq - the sequent to which this reasoner is applied
      reasonerInput - the input to this reasoner
      pm - a proof monitor (provides cancellation)
      Returns:
      the result of applying this reasoner to the given sequent (either a proof rule or a failure)
      See Also:
      • IProofRule
      • IReasonerFailure

    • hypReasonerFailure

      private IReasonerOutput hypReasonerFailure(AbstractManualRewrites.Input input, IPosition position, Predicate hyp)

    • goalReasonerFailure

      private IReasonerOutput goalReasonerFailure(AbstractManualRewrites.Input input, IPosition position, Predicate goal)

    • getNeededHyps

      protected Set<Predicate> getNeededHyps(IProverSequent seq, Predicate pred, IPosition position)
      Returns the list of hypotheses needed to perform rewriting.
      Parameters:
      seq - seq the sequent to which this reasoner is applied
      pred - the predicate to be rewriten
      position - the position of the predicate in the formula tree
      Returns:
      the needed hypotheses to apply the rewriting, an empty set if no hypotheses are needed, or null if the rewriting can not be applied.

    • rewrite

      public abstract Predicate rewrite(Predicate pred, IPosition position)
      Apply the rewriting to the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - predicate to rewrite
      Returns:
      the predicate which is the result of rewriting

    • getDisplayName

      protected abstract String getDisplayName(Predicate pred, IPosition position)
      Returns the name to display in the generated rule.
      Parameters:
      pred - the hypothesis predicate that gets rewritten or null if it is the goal that gets rewritten
      Returns:
      the name to display in the rule

    • getHypActions

      protected List<IHypAction> getHypActions(Predicate hyp, IPosition position, Predicate inferredHyp, FormulaFactory ff)
      Returns the actions to perform on hypotheses.

      Implementations may override this method to perform more hypothesis actions.

      Parameters:
      hyp - the hypothesis predicate that gets rewritten
      inferredHyp - the inferred hypothesis after rewrite occurred
      Returns:
      the actions to perform on hypotheses

    • deserializeInput

      public final IReasonerInput deserializeInput(IReasonerInputReader reader) throws SerializeException
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Deserializes an input of this reasoner using the given reader.

      Clients should retrieve from the reader all data needed to reconstruct an input that was previously serialized. The reader provides means to access to data serialized explicitly, as well as to the proof rule that was generated from the serialized input.

      The returned value must not be null.

      Specified by:
      deserializeInput in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      reader - the reader to use
      Returns:
      a new instance of the input that was previously serialized
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.

    • serializeInput

      public final void serializeInput(IReasonerInput input, IReasonerInputWriter writer) throws SerializeException
      Description copied from interface: IReasoner
      Serializes the given input using the given writer.

      Clients should store with the given writer all data of the input that can not be retrieved from the proof rule that was generated by the last successful application of this reasoner to a sequent.

      Specified by:
      serializeInput in interface IReasoner
      Parameters:
      input - the input to serialize
      writer - the writer to use
      Throws:
      SerializeException - In case of error when serializing.
